name: Jvedio Electron Desktop Refactor
overview: Switch the active UI refactor track from WPF wireframe delivery to Electron front-end planning with a C# Worker backend, and persist the documentation plus tracking system before implementation.
content: The new active route keeps Home as media-library management, adds Actors as a first-class left-nav page, keeps Settings as a shell entry, reuses the current C# scan/scrape/database/playback capabilities through a future Worker process, and treats fntv-electron as a shell-and-page reference only.

todolist.content: Rewrite the active feature handoff, plan, validation, and implementation documents for the Electron route
Add stable Electron product and page specification docs under doc/UI/desktop-ui-shell-refactor/electron
Add fntv-electron reference notes and mark old WPF wireframes as historical references
Clone D:/study/Proj/fntv-electron and enrich the reference notes with local source observations
Freeze the Worker API draft, page flows, and test strategy before code implementation
Align page specs with the renderer component and section boundaries before implementation
Freeze contract groups, DTO naming, task payload naming, and error code prefixes
Prepare the first implementation batch for Home library CRUD and left navigation sync
Stage C-1: Create contracts plus Electron and Worker project skeletons for Home MVP
Stage C-2: Implement bootstrap, libraries, create/delete, and tasks summary APIs
Stage C-3: Implement Home page flow, dialogs, and left navigation sync in renderer
Stage C-4: Add event consumption, task summary refresh, and structured error handling

openQuestions: Whether the future Worker should use a fixed localhost port or a runtime-selected port.
Whether a dedicated task center page should ship in the first Electron implementation batch.
